_comment: Documentation overlay for luna.* functions that cannot be documented via standard /// docstrings (e.g., functions registered inside closure bodies). Format: { 'module.funcName': { 'description': '...', 'params': [...], 'returns': '...' } }

particle.setSpread.description: Sets the emission spread angle for new particles.

particle.getSpread.description: Returns the emission spread angle in radians.
particle.getSpread.returns: number — The spread angle in radians.

particle.setLinearAcceleration.description: Sets the linear acceleration range applied to each particle per second.

particle.getLinearAcceleration.description: Returns the linear acceleration range as (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax).
particle.getLinearAcceleration.returns: number, number, number, number — xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax.

particle.setRadialAcceleration.description: Sets the radial acceleration range applied toward or away from the emitter.

particle.getRadialAcceleration.description: Returns the radial acceleration range (min, max).
particle.getRadialAcceleration.returns: number, number — min, max radial acceleration.

particle.setTangentialAcceleration.description: Sets the tangential acceleration range applied perpendicular to the radial direction.

particle.getTangentialAcceleration.description: Returns the tangential acceleration range (min, max).
particle.getTangentialAcceleration.returns: number, number — min, max tangential acceleration.

particle.setLinearDamping.description: Sets the linear damping range that slows particle velocity over time.

particle.getLinearDamping.description: Returns the linear damping range (min, max).

particle.setSizes.description: Sets the particle size keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic numbers.

params.desc: One or more size values sampled over the particle lifetime.

particle.getSizes.description: Returns the particle size keyframes as a table of numbers.

particle.setSizeVariation.description: Sets the random size variation factor applied on spawn, clamped to 0–1.

particle.setRotation.description: Sets the initial rotation range for new particles in radians.

particle.getRotation.description: Returns the initial rotation range (min, max) in radians.
particle.getRotation.returns: number, number — min, max rotation in radians.

particle.setSpin.description: Sets the angular velocity (spin) range for particles in radians per second.

particle.getSpin.description: Returns the spin range (min, max) in radians per second.
particle.getSpin.returns: number, number — min, max spin in radians/s.

particle.setSpinVariation.description: Sets the random spin variation factor applied on spawn, clamped to 0–1.

particle.setRelativeRotation.description: Enables or disables particle rotation relative to its velocity direction.

params.name: enable
params.desc: true to rotate particles along their travel direction.

particle.hasRelativeRotation.description: Returns whether relative rotation is currently enabled.
particle.hasRelativeRotation.returns: boolean — true if relative rotation is active.

particle.setColors.description: Sets color keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic {r, g, b, a} tables.

params.desc: One or more {r, g, b, a} color tables sampled over the particle lifetime.

particle.getColors.description: Returns the color keyframes as an array of {r, g, b, a} tables.
particle.getColors.returns: table — Array of {r, g, b, a} tables.

particle.setTexture.description: Assigns a texture to use when rendering particles.

particle.getTexture.description: Returns the texture handle assigned to this particle system, or nil if none is set.
particle.getTexture.returns: integer | nil — Texture handle or nil.

particle.setOffset.description: Sets the draw offset applied to each particle relative to the emitter origin.

particle.getOffset.description: Returns the draw offset (ox, oy) in pixels.
particle.getOffset.returns: number, number — ox, oy offset in pixels.

particle.setInsertMode.description: Sets how new particles are inserted into the buffer: "top", "bottom", or "random".

particle.getInsertMode.description: Returns the current particle insertion mode string.

particle.setBufferSize.description: Sets the maximum number of live particles the buffer can hold.

particle.getBufferSize.description: Returns the maximum particle buffer capacity.

particle.setQuads.description: Sets the sub-image quads used when the texture contains animated particle frames.

params.name: quads
params.desc: Array of {x, y, w, h} sub-image rectangles.

particle.setGravity.description: Sets the gravity vector applied to each particle every simulation step.

particle.getGravity.description: Returns the gravity vector (gx, gy) in pixels per second squared.
particle.getGravity.returns: number, number — gx, gy gravity components.

particle.setAlphas.description: Sets alpha transparency keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic numbers (0–1).

params.desc: One or more alpha values (0–1) sampled over the lifetime.

particle.getAlphas.description: Returns the alpha keyframes as a table of numbers (0–1).

particle.setEmissionShape.description: Sets the area shape from which new particles are spawned. Supported shapes: "point", "circle", "rectangle", "ring", "line", "cone", "star", "spiral".

params.name: args
params.desc: Optional shape parameter table (radius, width, height, etc.).

particle.getEmissionShape.description: Returns the current emission shape name and its parameter table.
particle.getEmissionShape.returns: string, table — Shape name and parameters.

particle.setRelativeMode.description: Sets whether particles stay attached to the emitter ("attached") or detach on spawn ("detached").

particle.newSystem.description: Creates and returns a new particle system with optional initial configuration.

params.name: config
params.desc: Optional config table. Keys: maxParticles, emissionRate, lifetimeMin, lifetimeMax, speedMin, speedMax, direction, spread, gravityX, gravityY, etc.

particle.update.description: Advances the particle simulation by dt seconds, spawning and aging particles.

particle.draw.description: Submits draw commands for all live particles at the given world position.

particle.stop.description: Stops particle emission and clears all live particles.

particle.pause.description: Pauses particle emission without clearing live particles.

particle.reset.description: Resets the emitter to its initial state, clearing all live particles.

particle.emit.description: Immediately emits the given number of particles, bypassing the emission rate.

params.name: count
params.desc: Number of particles to emit instantly.

particle.clone.description: Creates a deep copy of the particle system with a fresh simulation state.
particle.clone.returns: ParticleSystem — New independent system with the same configuration.

particle.release.description: Frees the particle system and all its resources, invalidating the handle.

particle.getCount.description: Returns the number of currently live particles in this system.

particle.isActive.description: Returns true if the emitter is actively emitting particles.
particle.isActive.returns: boolean — true if the emitter is running.

particle.isPaused.description: Returns true if the emitter is currently paused.

particle.isStopped.description: Returns true if the emitter is stopped.

particle.isEmpty.description: Returns true if there are no live particles in the system.
particle.isEmpty.returns: boolean — true if particle count is zero.

particle.isFull.description: Returns true if the particle buffer is at maximum capacity.
particle.isFull.returns: boolean — true if at buffer limit.

particle.getPosition.description: Returns the emitter world position (x, y).
particle.getPosition.returns: number, number — x, y world coordinates.

particle.moveTo.description: Moves the emitter to (x, y), smoothly adjusting in-flight particles.

particle.setEmissionRate.description: Sets the continuous emission rate in particles per second.

particle.getEmissionRate.description: Returns the continuous emission rate in particles per second.

particle.setEmissionArea.description: Sets the area distribution, dimensions, and angle for the particle spawn area.

params.name: angle
params.desc: Area rotation angle in radians (optional, default 0).

params.name: relative
params.desc: Whether direction is relative to area angle (optional, default false).

particle.getEmissionArea.description: Returns the emission area settings (distribution, w, h, angle, relative).
particle.getEmissionArea.returns: string, number, number, number, boolean — distribution, width, height, angle, relative.

particle.setParticleLifetime.description: Sets the minimum and maximum lifetime for each particle in seconds.

particle.getParticleLifetime.description: Returns the particle lifetime range (min, max) in seconds.
particle.getParticleLifetime.returns: number, number — min, max lifetime in seconds.

particle.setEmitterLifetime.description: Sets the emitter total lifetime in seconds. Use -1 for infinite.

params.name: lifetime
params.desc: Lifetime in seconds, or -1 for infinite.

particle.getEmitterLifetime.description: Returns the emitter lifetime in seconds (-1 means infinite).

particle.setSpeed.description: Sets the initial particle speed range in pixels per second.

particle.getSpeed.description: Returns the particle speed range (min, max) in pixels per second.
particle.getSpeed.returns: number, number — min, max speed in pixels/s.

particle.setDirection.description: Sets the emission direction in radians (0 = right, π/2 = down).

particle.getDirection.description: Returns the emission direction in radians.

gui.setOnChange.description: Sets the callback function invoked when the widget value changes.

params.name: fn
params.desc: Callback called with the new value.

gui.setOnDraw.description: Sets a custom draw callback invoked every frame to render additional content over the widget.

gui.containsPoint.description: Returns true if the given screen coordinates lie within the widget's bounding rectangle.
gui.containsPoint.returns: boolean — true if the point is inside the widget.

gui.setPadding.description: Sets the inner padding of the widget in pixels.

gui.getPadding.description: Returns the widget padding as (top, right, bottom, left) in pixels.
gui.getPadding.returns: number, number, number, number — top, right, bottom, left.

gui.setMargin.description: Sets the outer margin of the widget in pixels.

gui.getMargin.description: Returns the widget margin as (top, right, bottom, left) in pixels.
gui.getMargin.returns: number, number, number, number — top, right, bottom, left.

gui.setZOrder.description: Sets the z-order (draw depth) of the widget. Higher values appear on top.

gui.getZOrder.description: Returns the z-order value of the widget.

gui.setMinSize.description: Sets the minimum size constraint for the widget in pixels.

gui.getMinSize.description: Returns the minimum size constraint (w, h) in pixels.
gui.getMinSize.returns: number, number — min width, min height.

gui.setMaxSize.description: Sets the maximum size constraint for the widget in pixels.

gui.getMaxSize.description: Returns the maximum size constraint (w, h) in pixels.
gui.getMaxSize.returns: number, number — max width, max height.

gui.setAnchor.description: Sets the anchor point as a fraction of the parent container (0–1 for each axis).

gui.setAnchorCenter.description: Centers the widget anchor (sets both axes to 0.5).

gui.clearAnchor.description: Removes the anchor constraint, restoring normal layout flow.

gui.setFlexGrow.description: Sets the flex-grow factor controlling how much extra space the widget absorbs.

gui.setFlexShrink.description: Sets the flex-shrink factor controlling how much the widget shrinks when space is tight.
Sets the flex-shrink factor controlling how much the widget shrinks when space is tight.

gui.getPosition.description: Returns the screen position (x, y) of the widget in pixels.
gui.getPosition.returns: number, number — x, y screen position.

gui.getSize.description: Returns the current computed size (w, h) of the widget in pixels.
gui.getSize.returns: number, number — width, height in pixels.

gui.isVisible.description: Returns true if the widget is currently visible.

gui.setEnabled.description: Enables or disables the widget, blocking user interaction when disabled.

params.name: enabled
params.desc: true to enable, false to disable.

gui.isEnabled.description: Returns true if the widget is currently enabled for user interaction.

gui.getId.description: Returns the unique integer identifier assigned to this widget.

gui.setTooltip.description: Sets the tooltip string displayed on hover.

params.name: text
params.desc: Tooltip text to show on hover.

gui.getTooltip.description: Returns the tooltip string, or nil if none set.
gui.getTooltip.returns: string | nil — Tooltip text or nil.

gui.getChildCount.description: Returns the number of direct child widgets contained in this widget.

pathfinding.FlowField.getWidth.description: Returns the width of the flow field grid in tiles.

pathfinding.FlowField.getDirection.description: Returns the flow direction vector (dx, dy) at the given tile coordinates.
pathfinding.FlowField.getDirection.returns: number, number — Flow direction dx, dy (unit vector).

pathfinding.FlowField.getDirectionAngle.description: Returns the flow direction as an angle in radians at the given tile coordinates.
pathfinding.FlowField.getDirectionAngle.returns: number — Angle in radians (0 = right, π/2 = down).

pathfinding.FlowField.getCostToTarget.description: Returns the navigation cost from the given tile to the nearest target.
pathfinding.FlowField.getCostToTarget.returns: number — Cost to nearest target, or math.huge if unreachable.

pathfinding.FlowField.isCalculated.description: Returns true if the flow field has been fully calculated and is ready for queries.
pathfinding.FlowField.isCalculated.returns: boolean — true if the field is calculated.

pathfinding.FlowField.getTargets.description: Returns the list of target tile coordinates used when the field was last calculated.
pathfinding.FlowField.getTargets.returns: table — Array of {x, y} target tile tables.

pathfinding.FlowField.hasGoal.description: Returns true if a single goal tile has been set via setGoal.
pathfinding.FlowField.hasGoal.returns: boolean — true if a goal tile is set.

pathfinding.FlowField.setGoal.description: Sets the goal tile coordinates and triggers flow field recalculation.

data.DataView.getUInt8.description: Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getInt8.description: Reads a signed 8-bit integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getInt16.description: Reads a signed 16-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getUInt16.description: Reads an unsigned 16-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getInt32.description: Reads a signed 32-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getUInt32.description: Reads an unsigned 32-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getFloat.description: Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 float at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getDouble.description: Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double at the given byte offset.

data.DataView.getSize.description: Returns the total size of the underlying data buffer in bytes.

pathfinding.FlowField.getHeight.description: Returns the height of the flow field grid in tiles.

pathfinding.FlowField.getGoal.description: Returns the goal tile coordinates (x, y) that the flow field was computed toward.
pathfinding.FlowField.getGoal.returns: integer, integer — Goal x, y tile coordinates.

pathfinding.FlowField.getDistance.description: Returns the navigation distance (cost) from the given tile to the goal, or nil if unreachable.
pathfinding.FlowField.getDistance.returns: number | nil — Distance/cost to goal, or nil if unreachable.

pipeline.PipelineStep.getType.description: Returns the step type identifier string for this pipeline step.

savegame.save.description: Serializes the given table and saves it to a named save slot.

params.name: slot
params.desc: Save slot name (used as part of the filename).

params.name: data
params.desc: Data table to serialize and save.
